steps.title: Identify the cost source
steps.content: The passage contrasts a short, straightforward marketing study with HILDA, a longitudinal study tracking economic well-being trends that can take a long time to develop. The funding burden comes from having to collect data over many years.

steps.title: Reject side issues
steps.content: Choice A explains why marketing studies may be easier, not why HILDA is expensive. Choice B questions whether the sample is large enough, which is not stated. Choice D shifts to long-term marketing trends, not economic well-being.

steps.title: Connect trends and time
steps.content: The remaining choice explains that long-term economic trends are hard to find without collecting data over many years. The answer is C.
